calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-08-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that there was a concern with the terms of the trust regarding the Trustee's power to invest the trust property in paragraph E and the limit on liability of the Trustee in paragraph G. Mr. Der explained that the requested powers came from the amended trust and stated that those powers applied to the subtrusts. Furthermore, Mr. Der stated that it was the intent for the accounts to appear as Custodial accounts. The Court will not approve the limit on liabilty of the Trustee. The Court allowed the petition, signing the modified order for the same in open court striking the language in paragraph G page 5.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-21-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch. Paul Kelly for Enes Willims and Frederick Bomben.

The Court stated that it reviewed all the papers and that everything appeared to be in order except for the four Minor Beneficiarie's shares which are to remain in the Trust. Mr. Der stated that the Third Account had been amended to address this issue with a Petition For Instructions. The Court granted the petition as to all requested distributions except for the four Minor's shares. The Court modified the order striking the section pertaining to the Minors' shares and signed the modified order in open Court. A total of $741,000.00 plus statutory interest is to be distributed. The Petition For Instructions was continued to February 18, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Ex Parte Application For Order Shortening Time For Notice And Advance Of Hearing Date For Ex Parte Application And Stipulation For An Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Order
Off Calendar Dec-18-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Pitts for Carolann Brlecic. Paul Kelly for Enes Williams and Frederick Bomben.

Mr. Pitts stated that a settlement was reached and that it was in the best interest of all parties. The Court informed Counsel that it would shorten time, but that it would not here the petition today. The hearing on the Petition For Approval Of The Stipulation And For An Order Approving The Settlement was advanced from March 4, 2009 to January 7, 2009, (order for the same signed in open court). Oppositions if any, are due by December 31, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Amended Petition
Off Calendar Jun-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Pitts for amd with Carolann Brlecic. Paul Kelly for the Petitioner.

Mr. Kelly stated that they were in the middle of taking depositions and in hopes of having a fruitful mediation at the end of the discovery process. Mr. Pitts suggested having the matter set for trial in October and stated that it would be helpful to accelerate things. The Court referred the matter to the department of the Presiding Judge to be set for trial, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: A. Santiago.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Amended Petition
Off Calendar May-28-2008 Continued To Jun-11-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Enes Williams and Frederick Bomben (Petitioners).

Ms. Harrington requested a two week continuance stating that the firm Attorney was not able to appear. She also stated that discovery had not been completed and that the matter would be ready for trial at the end of September. The matter was continued to June 11, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR#11332. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Handa.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Amended Petition
Off Calendar Mar-05-2008 Continued To May-28-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Pitts for Carolann Brlecic. Paul Kelly for Enes Willems and Frederick Bomben (Petitioners).

Mr. Pitts stated that mediation was unsuccessful, that they were proceeding with discovery, and that depositions were set for the end of March. In addition, he informed the Court that it would take about 4 months to complete discovery and about 3 days to try the matter. The matter was continued to May 28, 2008 for status on discovery and the possibility of setting the matter for trial.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

APPEARANCES- ATTY. JOHN ST. JOHN APPEARING FOR FRED BOMBEN; ATTY. MICHAEL PITTS, FOR THE RESPONDENT.

ATTY. ST. JOHN REPORTED TO THE COURT THAT MEDIATION DOES NOT LOOK GOOD. DISCOVERY IS NOT COMPLETED. ATTY. PITTS STATED HE WILL NEED FIVE MONTHS TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO MARCH 5, 2008 FOR STATUS AND AT THAT TIME THE COURT WILL SET A TRIAL DATE.

JUDGE: JOHN DEARMAN; REPORTER: KATH BERSAMIN, CSR, 1172; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; BAILIFF: FELICIA SMITH

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Amended Petition
Off Calendar Sep-18-2007 Continued To Dec-12-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Michael Pitts appearing for respondent Carolann Briecic. John St. John appearing for petitioner. Mr. St. John reported that he has taken the depostion of the respondent. He has more discovery to complete that this case will be ready for trial in two to three months. Court asked counsel if they will consider Pro Bono Mediation. Both counsel are willing to consider mediation. Court is referring this matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed not later than 12/5. Matter is continued to 12/12 for status conference. If the case is not settled, Court will set it for trial.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: F. Smith.
